LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY CLAIM AND AND DENIED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANTS DID NOT SATISFY THEIR BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO SHOW THAT MR. BRIONES DID NOT BREACH THE STANDARD OF CARE. MS. OCHOA TESTIFIED AT 20:12-22:11 OF HER DEPOSITION THAT SHE SAW PLAINTIFF PRIOR TO MR. BRIONES SLOWING DOWN. THE REASONABLE INFERENCE IS THAT MR. BRIONES BREACHED THE STANDARD OF CARE BY PROCEEDING THROUGH THE INTERSECTION WITHOUT SEEING OR PAYING ATTENTION TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THE LACK OF MERIT OF CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
